verses.latin_vulgate: Viro qui corripientem dura cervice contemnit,  repentinus ei superveniet interitus,  et eum sanitas non sequetur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִ֣ישׁ תּ֭וֹכָחוֹת מַקְשֶׁה־עֹ֑רֶף פֶּ֥תַע יִ֝שָּׁבֵ֗ר וְאֵ֣ין מַרְפֵּֽא׃
verses.king_james: He, that being often reproved hardeneth his neck, shall suddenly be destroyed, and that without remedy.

verses.latin_vulgate: In multiplicatione justorum lætabitur vulgus ;  cum impii sumpserint principatum, gemet populus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּרְב֣וֹת צַ֭דִּיקִים יִשְׂמַ֣ח הָעָ֑ם וּבִמְשֹׁ֥ל רָ֝שָׁ֗ע יֵאָ֥נַֽח עָֽם׃
verses.king_james: When the righteous are in authority, the people rejoice: but when the wicked beareth rule, the people mourn.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vir qui amat sapientiam lætificat patrem suum ;  qui autem nutrit scorta perdet substantiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִישׁ־אֹהֵ֣ב חׇ֭כְמָה יְשַׂמַּ֣ח אָבִ֑יו וְרֹעֶ֥ה ז֝וֹנ֗וֹת יְאַבֶּד־הֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: Whoso loveth wisdom rejoiceth his father: but he that keepeth company with harlots spendeth his substance.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rex justus erigit terram ;  vir avarus destruet eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֶ֗לֶךְ בְּ֭מִשְׁפָּט יַעֲמִ֣יד אָ֑רֶץ וְאִ֖ישׁ תְּרוּמ֣וֹת יֶהֶרְסֶֽנָּה׃
verses.king_james: The king by judgment establisheth the land: but he that receiveth gifts overthroweth it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Homo qui blandis fictisque sermonibus loquitur amico suo  rete expandit gressibus ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גֶּ֭בֶר מַחֲלִ֣יק עַל־רֵעֵ֑הוּ רֶ֝֗שֶׁת פּוֹרֵ֥שׂ עַל־פְּעָמָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: A man that flattereth his neighbour spreadeth a net for his feet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Peccantem virum iniquum involvet laqueus,  et justus laudabit atque gaudebit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּפֶ֤שַֽׁע אִ֣ישׁ רָ֣ע מוֹקֵ֑שׁ וְ֝צַדִּ֗יק יָר֥וּן וְשָׂמֵֽחַ׃
verses.king_james: In the transgression of an evil man there is a snare: but the righteous doth sing and rejoice.

verses.latin_vulgate: Novit justus causam pauperum ;  impius ignorat scientiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֹדֵ֣עַ צַ֭דִּיק דִּ֣ין דַּלִּ֑ים רָ֝שָׁ֗ע לֹא־יָבִ֥ין דָּֽעַת׃
verses.king_james: The righteous considereth the cause of the poor: but the wicked regardeth not to know it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Homines pestilentes dissipant civitatem ;  sapientes vero avertunt furorem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַנְשֵׁ֣י לָ֭צוֹן יָפִ֣יחוּ קִרְיָ֑ה וַ֝חֲכָמִ֗ים יָשִׁ֥יבוּ אָֽף׃
verses.king_james: Scornful men bring a city into a snare: but wise men turn away wrath.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vir sapiens si cum stulto contenderit,  sive irascatur, sive rideat, non inveniet requiem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִֽישׁ־חָכָ֗ם נִ֭שְׁפָּט אֶת־אִ֣ישׁ אֱוִ֑יל וְרָגַ֥ז וְ֝שָׂחַ֗ק וְאֵ֣ין נָֽחַת׃
verses.king_james: If a wise man contendeth with a foolish man, whether he rage or laugh, there is no rest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Viri sanguinum oderunt simplicem ;  justi autem quærunt animam ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַנְשֵׁ֣י דָ֭מִים יִשְׂנְאוּ־תָ֑ם וִ֝ישָׁרִ֗ים יְבַקְשׁ֥וּ נַפְשֽׁוֹ׃
verses.king_james: The bloodthirsty hate the upright: but the just seek his soul.

verses.latin_vulgate: Totum spiritum suum profert stultus ;  sapiens differt, et reservat in posterum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־ר֭וּחוֹ יוֹצִ֣יא כְסִ֑יל וְ֝חָכָ֗ם בְּאָח֥וֹר יְשַׁבְּחֶֽנָּה׃
verses.king_james: A fool uttereth all his mind: but a wise man keepeth it in till afterwards.

verses.latin_vulgate: Princeps qui libenter audit verba mendacii,  omnes ministros habet impios.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֹ֭שֵׁל מַקְשִׁ֣יב עַל־דְּבַר־שָׁ֑קֶר כׇּֽל־מְשָׁרְתָ֥יו רְשָׁעִֽים׃
verses.king_james: If a ruler hearken to lies, all his servants are wicked.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pauper et creditor obviaverunt sibi :  utriusque illuminator est Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רָ֤שׁ וְאִ֣ישׁ תְּכָכִ֣ים נִפְגָּ֑שׁוּ מֵ֤אִיר־עֵינֵ֖י שְׁנֵיהֶ֣ם יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: The poor and the deceitful man meet together: the Lord lighteneth both their eyes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rex qui judicat in veritate pauperes,  thronus ejus in æternum firmabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֶ֤לֶךְ שׁוֹפֵ֣ט בֶּאֱמֶ֣ת דַּלִּ֑ים כִּ֝סְא֗וֹ לָעַ֥ד יִכּֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: The king that faithfully judgeth the poor, his throne shall be established for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Virga atque correptio tribuit sapientiam ;  puer autem qui dimittitur voluntati suæ confundit matrem suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שֵׁ֣בֶט וְ֭תוֹכַחַת יִתֵּ֣ן חׇכְמָ֑ה וְנַ֥עַר מְ֝שֻׁלָּ֗ח מֵבִ֥ישׁ אִמּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: The rod and reproof give wisdom: but a child left to himself bringeth his mother to shame.

verses.latin_vulgate: In multiplicatione impiorum multiplicabuntur scelera,  et justi ruinas eorum videbunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּרְב֣וֹת רְ֭שָׁעִים יִרְבֶּה־פָּ֑שַׁע וְ֝צַדִּיקִ֗ים בְּֽמַפַּלְתָּ֥ם יִרְאֽוּ׃
verses.king_james: When the wicked are multiplied, transgression increaseth: but the righteous shall see their fall.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erudi filium tuum, et refrigerabit te,  et dabit delicias animæ tuæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַסֵּ֣ר בִּ֭נְךָ וִינִיחֶ֑ךָ וְיִתֵּ֖ן מַעֲדַנִּ֣ים לְנַפְשֶֽׁךָ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Correct thy son, and he shall give thee rest; yea, he shall give delight unto thy soul.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum prophetia defecerit, dissipabitur populus ;  qui vero custodit legem beatus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּאֵ֣ין חָ֭זוֹן יִפָּ֣רַֽע עָ֑ם וְשֹׁמֵ֖ר תּוֹרָ֣ה אַשְׁרֵֽהוּ׃
verses.king_james: Where there is no vision, the people perish: but he that keepeth the law, happy is he.

verses.latin_vulgate: Servus verbis non potest erudiri,  quia quod dicis intelligit, et respondere contemnit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּ֭דְבָרִים לֹא־יִוָּ֣סֶר עָ֑בֶד כִּי־יָ֝בִ֗ין וְאֵ֣ין מַעֲנֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: A servant will not be corrected by words: for though he understand he will not answer.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vidisti hominem velocem ad loquendum ?  stultitia magis speranda est quam illius correptio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חָזִ֗יתָ אִ֭ישׁ אָ֣ץ בִּדְבָרָ֑יו תִּקְוָ֖ה לִכְסִ֣יל מִמֶּֽנּוּ׃
verses.king_james: Seest thou a man that is hasty in his words? there is more hope of a fool than of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui delicate a pueritia nutrit servum suum  postea sentiet eum contumacem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְפַנֵּ֣ק מִנֹּ֣עַר עַבְדּ֑וֹ וְ֝אַחֲרִית֗וֹ יִהְיֶ֥ה מָנֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: He that delicately bringeth up his servant from a child shall have him become his son at the length.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vir iracundus provocat rixas,  et qui ad indignandum facilis est erit ad peccandum proclivior.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִֽישׁ־אַ֭ף יְגָרֶ֣ה מָד֑וֹן וּבַ֖עַל חֵמָ֣ה רַב־פָּֽשַׁע׃
verses.king_james: An angry man stirreth up strife, and a furious man aboundeth in transgression.

verses.latin_vulgate: Superbum sequitur humilitas,  et humilem spiritu suscipiet gloria.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּאֲוַ֣ת אָ֭דָם תַּשְׁפִּילֶ֑נּוּ וּשְׁפַל־ר֝֗וּחַ יִתְמֹ֥ךְ כָּבֽוֹד׃
verses.king_james: A man’s pride shall bring him low: but honour shall uphold the humble in spirit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui cum fure participat odit animam suam ;  adjurantem audit, et non indicat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חוֹלֵ֣ק עִם־גַּ֭נָּב שׂוֹנֵ֣א נַפְשׁ֑וֹ אָלָ֥ה יִ֝שְׁמַ֗ע וְלֹ֣א יַגִּֽיד׃
verses.king_james: Whoso is partner with a thief hateth his own soul: he heareth cursing, and bewrayeth it not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui timet hominem cito corruet ;  qui sperat in Domino sublevabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חֶרְדַּ֣ת אָ֭דָם יִתֵּ֣ן מוֹקֵ֑שׁ וּבוֹטֵ֖חַ בַּיהֹוָ֣ה יְשֻׂגָּֽב׃
verses.king_james: The fear of man bringeth a snare: but whoso putteth his trust in the Lord shall be safe.

verses.latin_vulgate: Multi requirunt faciem principis,  et judicium a Domino egreditur singulorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רַ֭בִּים מְבַקְשִׁ֣ים פְּנֵֽי־מוֹשֵׁ֑ל וּ֝מֵיְהֹוָ֗ה מִשְׁפַּט־אִֽישׁ׃
verses.king_james: Many seek the ruler’s favour; but every man’s judgment cometh from the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abominantur justi virum impium,  et abominantur impii eos qui in recta sunt via.  Verbum custodiens filius  extra perditionem erit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תּוֹעֲבַ֣ת צַ֭דִּיקִים אִ֣ישׁ עָ֑וֶל וְתוֹעֲבַ֖ת רָשָׁ֣ע יְשַׁר־דָּֽרֶךְ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: An unjust man is an abomination to the just: and he that is upright in the way is abomination to the wicked.
